title: iris florentina subsp. albicans
intro: Iris florentina subsp. albicans, also known as the white flowering iris, is a subspecies of the iris plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Iris florentina subsp. albicans include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including tinctures, teas, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
